title: rosa urbica var. trichoneura
intro: Rosa urbica var. trichoneura, also known as the wild rose or prickly rose, is a species of rose native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Rosa urbica var. trichoneura lie in its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against cell damage. Rosa urbica var. trichoneura can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of conditions from digestive issues to wounds and skin conditions.
